articles.title: Higher Child Opportunity Index is Associated With Supracondylar Humerus Fracture Rates in the Pediatric Population.
articles.authors: Luck C, Armet G, Feder N, Wittman S, Ray K, McCoy A
articles.population: Children aged 18 years or younger who underwent surgical fixation for supracondylar humerus fracture
articles.outcomes: Supracondylar humerus fracture rates across Child Opportunity Index levels; associations with obesity, insurance status, and treatment type (open versus closed reduction)
articles.take_home: Children living in higher opportunity neighbourhoods had higher rates of supracondylar humerus fractures, with an even greater increase for Black children in these areas. Obese children were more likely to have open fractures and to need open surgery rather than closed reduction.

articles.title: Lateral Pillar Predicts Success of Greater Trochanter Apophyseodesis in Legg-Calve-Perthes Disease.
articles.authors: Campa W, Price M, Tamai J, Mehlman CT
articles.population: Children with unilateral Legg-Calve-Perthes disease
articles.sample_size: 139 patients (73 underwent greater trochanter apophyseodesis, 66 controls)
articles.outcomes: Relative overgrowth of greater trochanter, absolute trochanteric growth inhibition, femoral neck morphology, articulotrochanteric distance, centre-trochanteric distance
articles.take_home: Greater trochanter apophyseodesis surgery reduced the overgrowth of the greater trochanter in children with Legg-Calve-Perthes disease, but children with the most severe form of the disease still had high rates of this problem despite surgery, suggesting they may need additional treatment.

articles.title: Internal Extramedullary Lengthening of the Femur With an Intramedullary Lengthening Nail: Outcomes and Technical Considerations From 2 Centers.
articles.authors: Nahm NJ, Georgiadis AG, Bafor A, Iobst CA, Dahl MT
articles.population: Young children undergoing femoral lengthening surgery
articles.outcomes: Complications, magnitude of lengthening achieved, comparison of antegrade versus retrograde techniques. Median follow-up 27 months. Median lengthening 3.8 cm (14% of femoral segment). 56% of cases had at least one complication, with 12 cases requiring reoperation.
articles.take_home: Motorized extramedullary femoral lengthening is a workable option for lengthening bones in young children, with complication rates similar to other femoral lengthening methods. Whether the surgeon approaches from the top or bottom of the femur made no significant difference to complication rates.

articles.title: Clinical accuracy and applications of large language models in pediatric orthopedics: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Qureshi I, Thomas N, Heffelfinger M, Murphy D
articles.study_type: Systematic review of artificial intelligence and large language model performance
articles.population: Pediatric orthopedic conditions including developmental dysplasia of the hip, slipped capital femoral epiphysis, and scoliosis
articles.sample_size: 15 studies included after screening of 2624 articles
articles.outcomes: Large language model accuracy ranged from 44.3% to 93% (pooled 74.1%); reproducibility moderate (Spearman coefficient 0.55); 33% of guideline-based responses rated neutral or inaccurate; reading complexity exceeded recommended level (grade 12.7); regional expert consensus varied significantly; 82% of parents trusted AI as supplementary tool
articles.take_home: Large language models like ChatGPT and Gemini can provide moderate accuracy in pediatric orthopedic information but are inconsistent with clinical guidelines, have poor readability for patients, and should only be used as educational aids under professional medical supervision, not for independent clinical decision-making.

articles.title: Short Leg Cast Versus Long Leg Cast Immobilization for Pediatric Tibia Fractures: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Sato A, Khair YJ, Pashou A, Nasr J, Bello AYB, Bulut HI, et al.
articles.study_type: Systematic review and meta-analysis of retrospective comparative studies
articles.population: Children with tibia fractures
articles.sample_size: 413 patients across 6 studies (163 short leg cast, 250 long leg cast)
articles.outcomes: Bone alignment (coronal and sagittal angulation), cast duration, complication rates, time to weight-bearing, and time to bone union
articles.take_home: Short leg casts and long leg casts worked similarly well for healing children's tibia fractures, with no difference in alignment or complications, but short leg casts allowed children to put weight on their leg and achieve bone healing slightly faster. However, the evidence is limited and more research is needed to confirm these findings.

articles.title: Factors Associated With Flat-Top Talus in Radiographically Evaluated Ponseti-Treated Clubfeet.
articles.authors: Galan-Olleros M, Samara E, Ahmed AM, Delayun C, Adebayo T, Younoszai M, et al.
articles.population: Children with idiopathic and non-idiopathic clubfoot treated with Ponseti method
articles.outcomes: Flat-top talus identified in 68% of feet; associated with higher baseline deformity severity, more casts needed, and prior surgery; no difference in relapse rates between groups
articles.take_home: Flat-top talus is common after Ponseti clubfoot treatment but appears to reflect how severe the original deformity was and how much treatment was needed, rather than causing the foot to relapse. Doctors can use these associations to monitor children and discuss long-term ankle movement limitations with families.

articles.title: Isolated First Rib Fractures in Adolescent Athletes: An Epidemiologic Review.
articles.authors: Walls HR, Shields C, Ellington MD, Edmonds EW
articles.population: Adolescent athletes under 18 years old with first rib fractures sustained during sport
articles.outcomes: Return to activities, strength recovery, time to presentation, physical therapy attendance
articles.take_home: All 13 young athletes with first rib fractures returned fully to sport without symptoms. Those with strength problems at the start all regained full strength, and conservative management with activity modification appeared safe and effective, whether or not physical therapy was used.

articles.title: Association between spasticity of the hip and development of hip displacement in children: a cohort study of 786 hips.
articles.authors: Bakhtiyar M, Iljazi A, Odgaard A, Wong C, Petersen MM, Balslev-Clausen A
articles.population: Non-ambulant children with cerebral palsy (GMFCS levels IV-V)
articles.outcomes: Hip displacement (migration percentage ≥30%) and association with hip spasticity measured by Modified Ashworth Scale
articles.take_home: Children with cerebral palsy who have higher spasticity in their hip muscles, particularly the hip adductors, are at greater risk of developing hip displacement. The risk increased substantially with higher spasticity levels.

articles.title: Management options and outcomes for Salter-Harris type 3 and 4 fractures of the proximal phalanx of the hallux in children.
articles.authors: Florian A, Gargiulo C, Facchi R, Marengo L, De Salvo S, Canavese F
articles.population: Paediatric patients with Salter-Harris type 3 and 4 fractures of the proximal phalanx of the hallux
articles.outcomes: Radiographic healing, range of motion, premature physeal closure, angular deformity, joint incongruity, infection
articles.take_home: Children with these fractures of the big toe can be treated effectively with closed reduction and K-wires, with all patients achieving good healing and near-normal movement without major complications. Early skeletal maturity in these cases means that premature physeal closure rarely causes lasting alignment problems.

articles.title: Preoperative Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction is Associated With Delayed Extubation After Surgical Correction of Neuromuscular Scoliosis.
articles.authors: Rahimzadeh JD, Weintraub MJ, Molokwu BO, Ahn DB, Kim H, Ogilvie D, et al.
articles.population: Children with neuromuscular scoliosis undergoing surgical correction
articles.outcomes: Delayed extubation (intubation for more than 24 hours); association between preoperative left ventricular ejection fraction and duration of intubation
articles.take_home: Children with lower heart pumping function (measured by ejection fraction on heart scans before surgery) were more likely to need breathing support for longer than 24 hours after neuromuscular scoliosis surgery. Heart scans before surgery may help identify which children are at higher risk and need extra preparation.

articles.title: The Coronal Lateral Collateral Ligament Sign Occurs Independent of Generalized Joint Hyperlaxity in Children and Adolescents With ACL-Deficient Knees.
articles.authors: Pedrick EG, Mitchell BC, Bomar JD, Edmonds EW
articles.population: Children and adolescents undergoing ACL reconstruction
articles.sample_size: 100 patients (87 normal joint laxity, 13 with generalised joint hyperlaxity)
articles.outcomes: Presence of coronal lateral collateral ligament sign on MRI and its association with generalised joint hyperlaxity, ramp lesions, and Segond fractures
articles.take_home: The LCL sign appears in about two-thirds of children with ACL tears regardless of whether they have generalised joint laxity or other knee injuries. This suggests the LCL sign may be a specific indicator of ACL damage rather than general looseness in the ligaments.

articles.title: Does Brace Weaning Matter in Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip? A Comparative Study of 2 Discontinuation Strategies Following Tubingen Splint Treatment.
articles.authors: Tzatzairis T, Pechlivanidou E, Zogakis P, Tsiridis E
articles.population: Children with developmental dysplasia of the hip (DDH) treated with Tubingen splint
articles.outcomes: Final alpha-angle measurement and acetabular index at 6 and 12 months; need for surgical intervention
articles.take_home: Gradual weaning of the splint over 3 months produced slightly better hip measurements than stopping it immediately, but the differences were small and all hips improved either way without needing surgery in either group.

articles.title: Proximal ulnar osteochondroma as a potential risk factor for radial head dislocation: A retrospective analysis.
articles.authors: Xiao H, Li M, Tan X, Tan Q, Ye W, Wu J, et al.
articles.population: Patients with hereditary multiple osteochondromas affecting the proximal radius and ulna
articles.outcomes: Radial head dislocation or subluxation, ulnar length measurements, ulnar bow measurements
articles.take_home: Tumours growing from the ulna bone in the upper forearm were associated with radial head dislocation when left untreated, whereas tumours on the radius side did not cause this problem. Surgical removal of proximal ulnar tumours may help prevent radial head dislocation.

articles.title: Straightening the facts: early versus late adolescent surgery in idiopathic scoliosis.
articles.authors: Choi JU, Lee CS, Lee DH, Cho JH, Park S, Je M, et al.
articles.population: Teenagers with adolescent idiopathic scoliosis undergoing posterior spinal fusion
articles.sample_size: 168 patients (37 under 14 years, 131 aged 14 years or older)
articles.outcomes: Radiographic curve correction rates, curve angles at follow-up, and Scoliosis Research Society-22 quality of life scores
articles.take_home: Patients who had scoliosis surgery before age 14 achieved better straightening of their spine that was better maintained over time compared to those operated on at age 14 or older, though both groups reported similar quality of life improvements.

articles.title: Twenty-Five Years of Global Private Investment in Pediatric Orthopaedic Start-ups.
articles.authors: Jordan J, Anari J, Cahill PJ, Younger J
articles.study_type: Financial analysis of private investment trends
articles.population: Pediatric and adult orthopaedic start-up companies
articles.sample_size: Investments tracked over 25 years from 2000 to 2024
articles.outcomes: Frequency and total amount of private capital investment in pediatric versus adult orthopaedic start-ups
articles.take_home: Pediatric orthopaedic start-ups receive far less private investment than adult orthopaedic companies, accounting for only 10% of all orthopaedic investments. However, when pediatric companies do receive backing, they are able to raise similar amounts of capital to adult-focused companies.

articles.title: Discrepancy Rates Between Skeletal Maturity Assessment Systems in Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Neo BXB, Choo JSH, Wong HK, Liu KPG, Jun-Hao T
articles.population: Adolescents with idiopathic scoliosis
articles.outcomes: Discrepancy rates between skeletal maturity assessment systems (Risser versus Sanders, Risser versus cervical vertebral maturation, and others)
articles.take_home: Different methods of assessing bone maturity in teenagers with scoliosis often disagree with each other, particularly the Risser method which tends to overestimate how mature the bones are. Using multiple assessment methods together may help doctors time treatment decisions more accurately.

articles.title: Clinical and functional outcomes of arthroscopic release with or without latissimus dorsi tendon transfer in obstetric brachial plexus palsy.
articles.authors: Kiratlioglu Y, Yalcin M, Toker M, Bezirgan U, Armangil M
articles.population: Children with obstetric brachial plexus palsy and shoulder contracture
articles.sample_size: 32 patients (18 in group A, 14 in group B)
articles.outcomes: Shoulder range of motion (external rotation, abduction, internal rotation) and functional test scores (hand-to-neck, hand-to-abdomen, hand-to-mouth)
articles.take_home: Both arthroscopic release alone and arthroscopic release with tendon transfer improved shoulder movement after birth injury to the nerve. Adding tendon transfer gave better improvement in shoulder abduction, and both approaches preserved internal rotation.

articles.title: Does the Location of the Distal Tibial Epiphyseal Fixation Affect Rod Failure in Children With Osteogenesis Imperfecta?
articles.authors: Wang K, Callister J, Wallace M
articles.population: Paediatric patients with osteogenesis imperfecta
articles.outcomes: Failure to telescope (rods not shortening properly as child grows) and time to failure
articles.take_home: Rods placed in the middle position on front-to-back X-rays were less likely to fail compared to rods placed to the side. Younger children had more failures, likely because their bones are still growing rapidly and are structurally weaker.

articles.title: Ideal Surgical Age for Distraction-Based Growth Friendly Techniques to Minimize Complications.
articles.authors: Beydemir A, Ibik S, Ramazanov R, Berktas M, Ayvaz M, Demirkiran HG, et al.
articles.population: Children with early-onset scoliosis treated with growing rods
articles.outcomes: Complication rates and severity; need for further surgery; relationship between age at surgery and complications
articles.take_home: Children who had surgery younger than 85 months had more complications overall, though not more severe ones, compared to those operated on at 85 months or older. Delaying surgery to 85 months or later may reduce complications in selected patients if the curve is not progressing too fast and the child's heart and lungs are stable.

articles.title: Minimal Clinically Important Difference (MCID) of the EOSQ-24 Questionnaire.
articles.authors: Park AM, Elfilali MM, Brown MW, Taha O, Weintraub MJ, Garg S, et al.
articles.study_type: Prospective multicenter study to determine minimal clinically important difference
articles.population: Children with early onset scoliosis and their parents
articles.outcomes: Minimal clinically important difference (MCID) for EOSQ-24 questionnaire; anchor-based MCID of 5.2 points and distribution-based MCID of 4.6 points
articles.take_home: A change of 5 points on the EOSQ-24 questionnaire represents a meaningful improvement in quality of life for children with early onset scoliosis. This threshold helps doctors recognise when treatment has made a real difference to how patients feel, not just what X-rays show.

articles.title: Guided growth: Current concepts and novel techniques for harnessing a child's growth potential.
articles.authors: Roelen MCR, van Bergen CJA, Siemensma MF, Eastwood DM, Sanpera I, Tolk JJ
articles.population: Children with limb malalignment and skeletal deformities
articles.outcomes: Correction of angular and rotational deformities in upper and lower extremities using guided growth techniques
articles.take_home: Guided growth is a less invasive way to correct children's bone deformities by using the child's own growth potential, rather than traditional surgery. It works by applying tension or compression to the growth plates to gradually straighten bones whilst minimising disruption to the developing skeleton.

articles.title: Naviculectomy combined with 'a la carte' soft-tissue releases: is it a practical solution for congenital vertical talus in adolescence?
articles.authors: Khamies MS, Mortada-Mahmoud A, Laklouk M
articles.population: Adolescents with neglected congenital vertical talus
articles.sample_size: 15 patients (11 boys, 4 girls; average age 10.67 years)
articles.outcomes: Functional improvement measured by Zorer score (2 to 11), pain reduction on visual analog scale (7 to 2), and radiographic measurements
articles.take_home: Removing the navicular bone combined with targeted soft-tissue releases significantly improved pain, function and foot alignment in adolescents with neglected congenital vertical talus, whilst reducing surgical complications compared to extensive soft-tissue dissection alone.

articles.title: A mini-review of risk factors for developmental dysplasia of the hip: insights from multiple comparative cohort studies.
articles.authors: Cho T, Nair A, Stokey P, Kaufmann K, Liu J
articles.population: Infants and children with and without developmental dysplasia of the hip
articles.outcomes: Relative risk of developmental dysplasia of the hip associated with breech presentation, family history, oligohydramnios, and female sex
articles.take_home: Babies born in breech position, those with a family history of hip dysplasia, girls, and those exposed to low amniotic fluid levels have three to four times higher risk of developing hip dysplasia. These findings support targeted screening for babies with these risk factors.

articles.title: Safety and efficacy of intraoperative vectored cranial traction with Gardner-Wells tongs for pediatric spine deformity surgery.
articles.authors: Sardesai KS, Georger L, Dumas A, Noe M, Schwend RM
articles.population: Children aged 2-18 years undergoing spine deformity surgery in prone position
articles.outcomes: Pressure-related complications including facial abrasions, pressure sores and ulcers, facial swelling, and perioperative vision loss
articles.take_home: Using Gardner-Wells tongs with angled traction during spine surgery in children significantly reduced pressure-related complications to 1.42%, with no cases of serious vision loss and all complications being minor and reversible. Longer surgery times were associated with higher complication rates.
